sense.definition: the general condition of a person's mind or body

examples.x: She scheduled a check-up to ensure her health was in good condition after feeling unwell for a few days.
Mental health is just as important as physical health and should be prioritized in our daily lives.
The government launched a campaign to promote health awareness in the community.
He decided to take a break from work to focus on his health and well-being.

sense.definition: a commitment by physicians to ethical principles in medical practice

examples.x: When I became a doctor, I recited the Hippocratic Oath as a promise to prioritize patient well-being.
The nurse was inspired by the principles of the Hippocratic Oath in her daily care.
Physicians often reflect on the Hippocratic Oath when faced with challenging ethical decisions.
The medical students took the Hippocratic Oath at their graduation ceremony.
As a medical practitioner, I strive to uphold the ideals of the Hippocratic Oath in my practice.

sense.definition: a period of time spent working for free or little pay in order to gain experience or to become qualified in a particular field
a field of study and profession focused on providing medical care, support, and advocacy for patients in various healthcare settings

examples.x: She is studying nursing at the local university.
Nursing requires both technical skills and compassion.
He decided to pursue a career in nursing to help others.
The college offers advanced courses in pediatric nursing.
Nursing is a rewarding profession that demands dedication and patience.

sense.definition: the professional work or business of a doctor, lawyer, dentist, or other experts providing services to clients or patients

examples.x: The lawyer decided to start her own practice after working for a large firm for several years.
The doctor moved his practice to a new location closer to the hospital.
After graduating from medical school, he joined a well-established practice with experienced physicians.

sense.definition: the written instructions of a doctor that allow the patient to get the medicines needed
‌medical and hospital services provided or paid for by the government for all the people in a community, nation, or district
the sorting of the sick or injured according to their need for emergency medical attention
the act of advising and authorizing the use of a medicine or treatment
a treatment or medication  for a certain disease or injury

examples.x: After years of research, they finally discovered a cure.
Scientists are working to find a cure for cancer.
The medicine provided relief, but it wasn't a permanent cure.
There is no known cure for the common cold.
Unfortunately, there is no quick cure for this illness.

sense.definition: any substance that is used for medicinal purposes

examples.x: Drugs prescribed by doctors play a crucial role in treating various medical conditions, from antibiotics for infections to painkillers for managing discomfort.
Herbal remedies, derived from plants, have been used for centuries as natural drugs to address a wide range of health issues, though their efficacy and safety can vary.
The pharmaceutical industry continually researches and develops new drugs to address emerging health challenges and improve patient outcomes.

sense.definition: a substance used for medical treatment or therapy to alleviate, cure, or prevent illness

examples.x: The doctor prescribed a medicament to relieve my pain.
I take a daily medicament to control my blood pressure.
The pharmacist provided a medicament for my allergy symptoms.
Mom gave me a medicament to reduce my fever.
I rely on a medicament to manage my chronic condition.

sense.definition: a treatment or medicine for a disease or to reduce pain that is not severe

examples.x: She tried various home remedies to alleviate her cold symptoms, including herbal teas and honey.
The doctor prescribed a new remedy to manage the patient's chronic back pain.
Natural remedies like acupuncture and massage therapy are gaining popularity as alternatives to traditional medicine.
It's important to follow the recommended dosage when using over-the-counter remedies to avoid adverse effects.
The herbalist suggested a remedy made from chamomile and lavender to promote relaxation and sleep.

sense.definition: a medical process in which a part of body is cut open to repair or remove a damaged organ

examples.x: The doctor explained the details of the operation to ensure the patient understood the procedure.
After the operation, the patient was monitored closely in the recovery room for any complications.
He underwent a successful operation to remove the appendix, and he was discharged the following day.
The surgical team performed the operation with precision, resulting in a smooth recovery for the patient.
Prior to the operation, the medical staff conducted several tests to assess the patient’s overall health.

sense.definition: the identification of the nature and cause of an illness or other problem

examples.x: The doctor provided a clear diagnosis after reviewing the test results.
Early diagnosis of the disease is crucial for effective treatment.
Her diagnosis revealed a rare autoimmune disorder.
The technician made a diagnosis of the equipment malfunction.
Accurate diagnosis requires a thorough examination and multiple tests.

sense.definition: a reason to avoid a specific medical treatment due to potential risks for the patient

examples.x: The doctor identified a contraindication, so I couldn't take that medication.
Due to a contraindication, the surgeon recommended an alternative procedure.
The nurse checked for contraindications before administering the vaccine.
A contraindication prompted the dentist to choose a different anesthesia method.
The pharmacist highlighted contraindications on the medication label.

sense.definition: a prescribed amount of medicine that is taken regularly

examples.x: The doctor adjusted the dosage to better manage her condition.
Always follow the prescribed dosage to ensure the medication's effectiveness.
He forgot his evening dosage of medication and needed to make up for it.
She set an alarm to remember her daily dosage of vitamins.
The pharmacist provided instructions on the correct dosage for the new prescription.

sense.definition: the action of putting a drug into a person's body using a syringe

examples.x: The nurse administered the flu vaccine with a quick injection into the patient's arm.
He winced slightly as the doctor gave him an injection to numb the area before stitching the wound.
Insulin injections are necessary for people with diabetes to manage their blood sugar levels.
The veterinarian gave the dog an injection to treat the infection in its paw.
The athlete received a pain-relieving injection before the game to manage a recurring injury.

sense.definition: a secondary effect of any drug or medicine, usually an undesirable one

examples.x: While the new medication effectively managed her symptoms, she was concerned about the potential side effects, including nausea and fatigue.
The doctor explained that every drug comes with the risk of side effects, which is why it's important to monitor patients closely after starting a new treatment.
After taking the antibiotic, he experienced a mild side effect in the form of an upset stomach, prompting him to call his healthcare provider for advice.
Researchers are currently studying the long-term side effects of the vaccine to ensure its safety and efficacy in various populations.
Although the pain reliever worked well for her headaches, she decided to stop taking it due to the unpleasant side effects that interfered with her daily activities.

sense.definition: the well-being of a person's mind

examples.x: They discussed strategies for improving mental health, such as mindfulness and regular exercise.
He attended a workshop on mental health to learn about coping mechanisms and self-care.
Mental health assessments are important for diagnosing and treating conditions like depression and bipolar disorder.
He attended therapy sessions to address his mental health concerns and improve his well-being.

sense.definition: an unexpected and mostly unpleasant effect of taking a drug, undergoing a medical treatment or procedure, etc.
something that we take to prevent or treat a disease, or to feel less pain

examples.x: He always keeps his asthma medication in his backpack.
He forgot to take his medication this morning.
Some medications can have side effects, so read the instructions carefully
The pharmacist explained how to take the medication correctly.
You shouldn't drink alcohol while on this medication.

sense.definition: someone who has studied medicine and treats sick or injured people

examples.x: I want to become a doctor so I can take care of people's health.
My mom is a doctor, and she helps people when they are sick.
The doctor asked me about my symptoms and medical history to make a diagnosis.
The doctor performed a surgery on my grandfather to remove his appendix.
We have an appointment with the doctor tomorrow morning for a check-up.

sense.definition: (medicine) the body's immune response against a transplanted organ or tissue, leading to an attempt to destroy or remove the foreign graft

examples.x: The patient experienced rejection after a kidney transplant.
Rejection is a risk in organ transplantation due to immune responses.
My friend's body showed signs of rejection following a heart transplant.
The doctor closely monitored for rejection symptoms after the surgery.
Rejection can occur when the immune system sees the transplant as foreign.

sense.definition: (medicine) someone who provides biological materials for transplantation or medical procedures

examples.x: My cousin became a kidney donor to help her brother.
The blood bank relies on regular donors for lifesaving transfusions.
She decided to be a tissue donor to assist with medical research.
My neighbor became a plasma donor to aid in medical treatments.

sense.definition: an operation performed by medical professionals to diagnose, treat, etc. a medical condition or injury

examples.x: The surgeon explained the details of the procedure scheduled for tomorrow to remove the appendix.
She underwent a minimally invasive procedure to repair a torn ligament in her knee.
Patients are often briefed on the risks and benefits of a procedure before giving consent.

sense.definition: a person who is allowed to use a medical facility
‌a vehicle specially equipped to take sick or injured people to a hospital

examples.x: The ambulance raced through the busy city streets, moving through traffic to reach the hospital as quickly as possible.
The ambulance pulled up in front of the hospital, and the paramedics quickly unloaded the patient.

sense.definition: the fact of being placed in a hospital for medical treatment

examples.x: After the accident, she required hospitalization for several weeks to recover from her injuries.
The doctor recommended immediate hospitalization due to the severity of his condition.
Hospitalization costs can vary depending on the length of stay and medical treatments required.
During his hospitalization, visitors were limited to immediate family members only.
The hospital provided excellent care during her hospitalization, ensuring she received round-the-clock attention.

sense.definition: a patient who stays in the hospital while they receive treatment

examples.x: She was admitted as an inpatient to manage her condition more effectively under constant medical supervision.
The doctor decided to admit him as an inpatient due to the severity of his condition.
He needed to stay as an inpatient for a few days to recover from his procedure.
The treatment plan required that she remain an inpatient to receive intravenous medication.
They scheduled regular check-ups for the inpatient to track progress and adjust treatment.

sense.definition: the condition of losing one's sensation or awareness during surgery and other procedures, particularly by use of special drugs

examples.x: The anesthesiologist monitored the patient's vital signs throughout the anesthesia.

sense.definition: a medical practice that involves cutting open a body part in order to repair, remove, etc. an organ

examples.x: After the surgery, the patient received instructions on how to care for the surgical wound.
The doctor recommended surgery to remove the tumor and improve the patient’s health.
She underwent surgery to repair a torn ligament in her knee.
The surgery was performed successfully, and the patient is expected to make a full recovery.
They scheduled the surgery for next week, following all necessary pre-operative tests.

sense.definition: a controlled scientific experiment in which the effectiveness and safety of a medical treatment is measured by testing it on people

examples.x: The new drug is currently undergoing a clinical trial to assess its safety and efficacy.
Participants in the clinical trial were monitored closely for any adverse reactions.
The results of the clinical trial will determine if the treatment can be approved for widespread use.
She volunteered for a clinical trial to help advance research for a potential cancer cure.
The clinical trial showed promising outcomes, with a significant improvement in patient recovery rates.

sense.definition: permission given by a patient to receive a particular treatment, informed of all the possible consequences and risks

examples.x: Before undergoing surgery, the patient signed a form indicating their informed consent after the surgeon explained the risks and benefits.
In medical research, participants must provide informed consent before they can join a study, understanding the procedures and potential risks involved.
The lawyer emphasized the importance of obtaining informed consent before proceeding with any legal action on behalf of the client.
The ethics committee reviewed the study protocol to ensure participants' rights were protected, including the requirement for informed consent.
Informed consent is a fundamental principle in medical ethics, ensuring patients have sufficient information to make informed decisions about their healthcare.

sense.definition: a method of administering a drug or other substance by a needle and syringe into the bloodstream
a place or period of separation in which someone or something that is suspicious of carrying a dangerous disease is kept away so that others can be safe
an action that is done to relieve pain or cure a disease, wound, etc.

examples.x: After the accident, he underwent extensive treatment to recover from his injuries and regain mobility.
The doctor prescribed a course of antibiotics as part of the treatment for the bacterial infection.
Physical therapy was a crucial aspect of her treatment plan to rehabilitate her injured knee.
The holistic approach to cancer treatment included chemotherapy, radiation therapy, and complementary therapies to support overall well-being.
Timely treatment of acute illnesses can prevent complications and facilitate a quicker recovery process.

sense.definition: the health services and  treatments given to people

examples.x: Access to affordable healthcare is a major concern for many families in the country.
The government introduced new policies to improve the quality of healthcare in rural areas.
She decided to pursue a career in healthcare because she wanted to help others.
The hospital is known for providing excellent healthcare to its patients.
Advances in technology have revolutionized modern healthcare, making treatments more effective and accessible.

sense.definition: a visit made to a patient or client in their own home by a doctor or other professional
the presence of cancerous cells with the potential to invade and spread

examples.x: My aunt underwent surgery to remove the malignancy in her breast.
The diagnostic test revealed the presence of malignancy in the lung.
The medical team closely monitored for signs of malignancy recurrence.
Early detection of malignancy allows for more effective cancer treatment.

sense.definition: the controlled administration of fluids or medications slowly through a tube directly into a patient's bloodstream

examples.x: The nurse set up a drip feed to administer the patient's medication.
Dad received a drip feed to regulate his antibiotic intake.
The continuous drip feed ensured a steady supply of pain relief.
During surgery, the doctor used a drip feed for intravenous hydration.
The hospital used a drip feed to administer chemotherapy to the cancer patient.

sense.definition: a substance or agent, such as certain foods, herbs, or drugs, that is believed to enhance or stimulate sexual desire, arousal, or performance
